region_of_origin: California, USA (cold-pressed juice movement originated in Los Angeles ~2010; arrived in Singapore via specialist juice bars ~2013)
food_subgroup: Cold-pressed green vegetable and fruit juice. Fibre loss: juice extraction removes almost all insoluble dietary fibre present in whole vegetables and fruit. High Vitamin K content (~390–420 mcg per 300 ml bottle) from kale and spinach.

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for cold-pressed green juice specifically. Closest parent concept: vegetable juice product (FOODON:03301737). Gap noted; null assigned pending FoodOn expansion.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cold-pressed kale spinach cucumber apple ginger green juice
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0JCG used as closest parent for vegetable/fruit juice blend; no exact code for cold-pressed multi-ingredient green juice exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for cold-pressed green juice. Closest clinical concept would be 'vegetable juice' but no exact code confirmed; null assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard retail bottle of cold-pressed green juice is approximately 300 ml (300 g), providing roughly 105 kcal, 2.7 g protein, 0.6 g fat, and 23.4 g carbohydrate per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Kale juice (FDC ID 168562), Spinach raw (FDC ID 168462), Cucumber raw (FDC ID 168409), Green apple raw (FDC ID 168203), Ginger root raw (FDC ID 169231); component calculation for cold-pressed juice blend
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived by weighted component calculation from USDA FoodData Central entries for each ingredient (kale, spinach, cucumber, green apple, ginger, lemon juice) at the stated gram weights per 300 ml bottle, then normalised to per-100 g basis. Cold-pressing retains most water-soluble nutrients; fibre is substantially lost in juice extraction. Values are consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for vegetable and fruit juices.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of fruit and vegetable juices — analogous estimate based on green apple juice GI (~40) and mixed vegetable juice GI data; University of Sydney GI Database reference for apple juice and vegetable juice blends
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific cold-pressed green juice blend. GI estimated at ~45 based on: (1) green apple juice GI ~40 (University of Sydney GI Database, apple juice entries); (2) vegetable juice blends (kale, spinach, cucumber) have low GI due to low sugar content and organic acid content from lemon; (3) fibre loss from juicing increases GI slightly vs whole fruit/vegetable. Weighted blend estimate yields GI ~45 (low category). GL per 300 ml serving = 45 × (23.4 g carb / 100) × (300/100) ≈ 9.9 (low-medium). Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (22 mg/100 g; ~66 mg per 300 ml bottle) combined with high potassium (~180 mg/100 g; ~540 mg per bottle) from kale, spinach, and cucumber
hypertension.guidance: Cold-pressed green juice is highly suitable for hypertension management: its negligible sodium content and high potassium load (~540 mg per bottle) promote renal sodium excretion and vasodilation, while dietary nitrates from kale and spinach are converted to nitric oxide, providing a modest additional blood-pressure-lowering effect.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic load (~9.9 per 300 ml bottle) but reduced dietary fibre (0.5 g/100 g vs ~2–3 g/100 g in whole vegetables) due to juice extraction accelerates sugar absorption relative to whole vegetables
type2_diabetes.guidance: Cold-pressed green juice has a low GL (~10 per bottle) and is generally suitable for Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions; however, patients should be aware that fibre loss from juicing means postprandial glucose rises faster than from equivalent whole vegetables or whole apple, and ginger gingerols may provide a modest insulin-sensitising benefit.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, high potassium, anti-inflammatory polyphenols (flavonols, lutein, beta-carotene from kale and spinach), and dietary nitrates supporting endothelial function
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cold-pressed green juice is cardioprotective: it contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, provides ~540 mg potassium per bottle supporting vascular health, and delivers anti-inflammatory carotenoids and flavonols from kale and spinach; patients on warfarin for cardiovascular indications must maintain consistent daily intake due to very high Vitamin K content (~390–420 mcg per bottle, approximately 3–4× the daily adequate intake).

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very high Vitamin K content (~390–420 mcg per 300 ml bottle) is clinically significant for RA patients on warfarin anticoagulation therapy
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis who are on warfarin must maintain strictly consistent daily intake of this juice due to its very high Vitamin K content (~390–420 mcg per bottle, 3–4× daily AI); anti-inflammatory gingerols from ginger (~50 mg per bottle) and polyphenols from kale and spinach provide mild additional anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cold-pressed green juice is a minimally processed raw beverage produced by hydraulic cold-pressing of fresh kale (~50 g), spinach (~30 g), cucumber (~80 g), green apple (~60 g), ginger (~10 g), and lemon juice (~20 ml) per 300 ml bottle, without heat pasteurisation. Cold-pressing uses hydraulic pressure at low temperature, generating less heat and oxidation than centrifugal juicing, thereby preserving more polyphenols, carotenoids, and heat-sensitive vitamins. Per 300 ml bottle: approximately 105 kcal, 2.7 g protein, 0.6 g fat, 23.4 g carbohydrate, 1.5 g dietary fibre, and 66 mg sodium. Nutritionally notable: very high Vitamin K (~390–420 mcg per bottle, approximately 3–4× the daily adequate intake) from kale and spinach — patients on warfarin anticoagulation must maintain strictly consistent daily volume to avoid INR fluctuation. High potassium (~540 mg per bottle) supports blood pressure management. Ginger provides approximately 50 mg gingerols per bottle with anti-nausea and mild anti-inflammatory properties. Glycemic index ~45 (low), glycemic load ~10 per bottle (low-medium). Key clinical caveat: juice extraction removes almost all insoluble dietary fibre present in whole vegetables, meaning sugars are absorbed faster than from equivalent whole vegetables or whole apple. The taste profile is typically fresh, slightly tart from lemon and apple, with a noticeable earthy green vegetable and spicy ginger undertone. Available at Singapore health cafés and specialty juice bars at approximately SGD 8–15 per 300 ml bottle. Suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free diets. The cold-pressed juice movement originated in Los Angeles health food culture (~2010) and arrived in Singapore via specialist juice bars from approximately 2013.
common_user_queries: cold pressed juice vs whole vegetables

confidence_reason: Nutrient values derived by weighted component calculation from USDA FoodData Central entries for each named ingredient at stated gram weights, normalised to per-100 g basis. USDA FoodData Central is an approved primary source. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries (apple juice, vegetable juice blends) with documented estimation basis. All values are internally consistent and plausible for a cold-pressed green vegetable and fruit juice blend.
notes: 1. FIBRE LOSS: Cold-pressing removes almost all insoluble dietary fibre; per-100 g dietary fibre of 0.5 g reflects juice form (vs ~2–3 g/100 g in whole kale/spinach). 2. VITAMIN K WARFARIN INTERACTION: ~390–420 mcg Vitamin K per 300 ml bottle (3–4× daily adequate intake); patients on warfarin must maintain consistent daily volume. 3. ZERO-VALUE JUSTIFICATION: saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central component data for this vegetable-fruit juice blend (no animal fats; plant lipids in kale/spinach are predominantly polyunsaturated but at negligible total fat levels). trans_fat_g is confirmed zero — no industrial trans fats in cold-pressed raw juice. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero — plant-based beverage, naturally zero cholesterol. vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed zero — plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients, naturally zero B12. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero — no significant vitamin D in kale, spinach, cucumber, apple, ginger, or lemon juice per USDA FoodData Central. 4. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this cold-pressed multi-ingredient juice blend not available as a composite in USDA FoodData Central; individual ingredient fatty acid data exist but composite blend fractionation not reported as a single entry. water_g set to -1: total moisture for the composite cold-pressed juice blend not directly reported in USDA FoodData Central as a composite entry; estimated ~90–92% water but not confirmed by a single approved source entry. 5. GI SOURCE: GI ~45 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (apple juice and vegetable juice blend references); no direct GI measurement for this exact recipe exists in the published literature. 6. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn URI null — no exact FoodOn term for cold-pressed green juice; closest parent is vegetable juice product. FoodEx2 A0JCG used as closest parent. SNOMED CT null — no confirmed code. 7. MULTILINGUAL: This food is sold in Singapore health cafés and is classified under Western Health cuisine; Singaporean multilingual naming rules applied (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Tamil and Hindi names are functional descriptive translations; no established single canonical term exists in these languages for this Western health product. 8. ALIASES CONSISTENCY: Self-check performed — every string value in aliases_by_language (english: 12 entries, chinese: 2 entries, malay: 1 entry, tamil: 2 entries, hindi: 2 entries, romanized: 1 entry) verified present verbatim in the flat aliases array. Total flat aliases: 20 entries.
